text.title: Project Integration Management — Agentic Workflow and Subagents
text.summary: Integrated-plan-as-code pipeline, subagent roles, change-impact prompt pattern, and AI-specific failure modes in integration contexts.

section.title: Integrated Plan as Code

rule.statement: Single source of truth: one YAML file (integrated/plan.yaml) in the project repo; derivatives (slides, PDFs, dashboards) are generated — never hand-edited.
rule.rationale: Manual edits to derivative documents diverge from the source within one week; agents that pull from the wrong derivative produce misleading status reports.

section.p: Charter (charter.md) lives in the same repo; sponsor approves via PR merge. A decisions/ directory holds one ADR-style entry per approved CR. This makes the program's decision history searchable.

list.item: charter-author-agent (opus): drafts charter from kickoff inputs, drives sponsor sign-off, captures assumptions and constraints.
change-impact-agent (sonnet): given a CR, computes impact across all knowledge areas from the integrated plan; emits a markdown impact memo.
baseline-curator-agent (sonnet): keeps the integrated plan consistent — flags scope without schedule, schedule without cost, risks without owners.
status-reporter-agent (haiku): renders the status report template from numeric inputs only; colour follows thresholds, never tone.
closure-agent (sonnet): runs the closure checklist, archives plans, captures lessons learned, generates final acceptance documentation.
password-scrubber-agent: runs over charter/status/closure docs before distribution; integration artifacts are top leak paths.

example.note: Strict markdown output forces numeric grounding; rejects vague recommendations:
example.code:
You are the change-impact agent. Inputs: 1. Approved change request (CR-NN.md): scope description, requestor, priority. 2. integrated/plan.yaml: scope (WBS), schedule, cost, risks, quality, resources. 3. Threshold table: GREEN/YELLOW/RED triggers per dimension.
Emit STRICT MARKDOWN: ## Impact Analysis CR-NN | Area | Current | Proposed | Delta | Status | Notes | |------|---------|----------|-------|--------|-------|
## Recommendation - decision: approve | reject | conditional - conditions: [ ... ] - new_baseline_required: true|false - communications: [ stakeholders to notify ]
Rules: - Numbers come from inputs; never invent. - Status follows threshold table verbatim. - Recommend reject if any dimension goes RED without a compensating trade-off. - Cite at least one risk-register entry if risk delta is non-zero.

antipattern.description: Agent produces cross-area impact analysis that only covers the areas it has API access to.
antipattern.reason: An agent with only Jira access produces schedule-blind reports. Force an input audit: every knowledge area must appear in the impact memo or be explicitly marked "data unavailable".

antipattern.description: Closure declared complete with an empty lessons-learned file.
antipattern.reason: Agents skip lessons learned because there is no code change. Force the closure checklist to fail-close if the lessons file is empty.

antipattern.description: Charter sign-off automated by the agent merging its own PR.
antipattern.reason: Charter sign-off is a governance decision requiring sponsor authority; the agent prepares the PR and the sponsor approves the merge.

list.item: Mandatory human checkpoints: charter approval, baseline changes, CR approval, status colour shifts to RED, closure declaration.
Decision log entries get duplicated by parallel agents — force a unique-ID generator and advisory lock.
Integrated plans over 500 lines blow context; page by knowledge area, persist summaries, rehydrate.
